title: Introduction to AI
overview: Artificial Intelligence is everywhere — from spam filters to voice assistants. Learn what AI is, how it relates to ML and Deep Learning, and where you'll encounter it daily.

blocks.title: What is Artificial Intelligence?
blocks.body: **Artificial Intelligence (AI)** is the field of computer science focused on building systems that perform tasks normally requiring human thinking — learning, reasoning, recognizing patterns, and making decisions.
bullets: Computers performing tasks that need human intelligence
Examples: playing chess, recognizing faces, translating languages
AI powers spam filters, voice assistants, and recommendation systems
Not about robots — about intelligent problem-solving

blocks.title: Think of AI as a Very Fast, Tireless Assistant
blocks.text: Imagine an assistant who never sleeps, processes millions of examples instantly, and follows rules or learns from examples. That's AI — you give it data or instructions, and it performs tasks intelligently.

centerPanel.label: Programming Concept
centerPanel.textContent:
Computers performing tasks that require human-like thinking:
• Playing chess
• Recognizing faces
• Filtering spam
• Recommending movies

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code:
# AI in daily life
# Gmail spam filter
# Google Maps routing
# Netflix recommendations
# Siri / Alexa
print('AI is everywhere!')

blocks.question: Is a basic calculator an AI system? Why or why not?
blocks.answer: No. A calculator follows fixed programmed rules (add, subtract). AI adapts, learns, or makes decisions beyond explicit programming. A spam filter IS AI because it learns from examples.
blocks.followupCode:
# Calculator: NOT AI
result = 5 + 3

# Spam filter: IS AI
# Learns from examples, adapts to new spam

blocks.title: AI vs Machine Learning vs Deep Learning
blocks.body: These three terms are related but describe different levels of the AI field.
bullets: **AI** — broadest umbrella: any technique making computers appear intelligent
**Machine Learning (ML)** — subset of AI: learns rules from data instead of being programmed
**Deep Learning (DL)** — subset of ML: uses brain-like neural networks with many layers

blocks.title: Think of ML as Teaching a Dog Tricks
blocks.text: You don't write every rule — you show examples and reward correct behavior. The dog learns patterns. ML works the same way: show data, let the computer find patterns.

centerPanel.label: Nested Categories
centerPanel.textContent:
AI (broadest)
  └── Machine Learning
        └── Deep Learning (neural networks)

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code:
# Hierarchy check
term = 'neural network layers'
if 'layer' in term:
    print('Deep Learning')
    print('Also ML and AI')
rightPanel.output:
Deep Learning
Also ML and AI

blocks.title: Narrow AI vs General AI
blocks.body: All AI today is **Narrow AI** — it excels at one specific task. **General AI** (capable of any intellectual task) remains theoretical.
bullets: Narrow AI: chess engines, spam filters, voice assistants
General AI: would excel at any task like a human — we don't have this yet
Every AI system you use today is Narrow AI

blocks.title: Think of DL as Layers in the Human Eye
blocks.text: Your eye has layers of cells — each layer detects edges, shapes, then objects. Deep Learning uses similar layered processing to understand complex data like images.

leftPanel.label: Real Life Analogy
leftPanel.title: The Specialist
leftPanel.description: Excels at one task, can't do everything

centerPanel.label: Today's AI
centerPanel.textContent:
Narrow AI (Today):
✓ Chess engine
✓ Spam filter
✓ Voice assistant
✓ Face recognition

General AI: Not yet!

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code:
# Narrow AI example
ai_task = 'recommend movies'
# Can ONLY do this one task well
print(f'AI specializes in: {ai_task}')

blocks.question: Which is the broadest term: AI, ML, or DL?
blocks.answer: AI is the broadest. ML is a subset of AI. DL is a subset of ML. Think of nesting dolls — AI contains ML contains DL.

blocks.title: AI in the Real World
blocks.body: AI isn't future technology — it's already embedded in tools you use every day.
bullets: **Spam filters** — learn which emails are junk from millions of examples
**Google Maps** — uses search and optimization to find fastest routes
**Netflix** — recommends shows using machine learning on your history
**Voice assistants** — understand speech using NLP and ML

rightPanel.label: Code Example
rightPanel.code:
# Step 1: Collect data
# Step 2: Train a model
# Step 3: Make predictions
print('Data → Model → Prediction')

leftPanel.label: Step 1
leftPanel.title: Collect Data
leftPanel.description: Gather examples the AI can learn from

rightPanel.label: Step 3
rightPanel.title: Use AI
rightPanel.description: The trained model makes intelligent decisions on new inputs

blocks.title: The CS50 AI Journey
blocks.body: This course covers 7 core AI topics you'll explore in upcoming lectures.
bullets: **Search** — finding paths from start to goal (GPS, puzzles)
**Knowledge** — representing facts and reasoning with logic
**Uncertainty** — handling probability when information is incomplete
**Optimization** — finding the best solution among many options
**Learning** — ML from labeled and unlabeled data
**Neural Networks** — brain-inspired computing for deep learning
**Language** — NLP and large language models like GPT

blocks.question: Which AI topic would a voice assistant like Alexa use?
blocks.answer: Multiple! NLP to understand speech, ML to personalize responses, Search to find answers, and Uncertainty to handle ambiguous requests. Real AI combines many techniques.

blocks.title: Identify AI Around You
blocks.instructions: Write a Python program that prints 3 AI tools you use daily and one sentence about what each does.
blocks.starterCode:
# List 3 AI tools you use
print('1. ')
print('2. ')
print('3. ')
blocks.solution:
print('1. Google Maps - finds fastest routes using search')
print('2. Spotify - recommends songs using ML')
print('3. Gmail - filters spam using AI')
blocks.hint: Think about apps on your phone. Each print() should name the tool and its AI task.

keyTakeaways.text: AI = computers doing tasks that need human thinking
ML is a subset of AI that learns from data
Deep Learning uses neural networks inspired by the brain
All AI today is Narrow AI — it does one specific task
AI is already in your daily life: maps, music, email filters
The CS50 AI path covers: Search, Knowledge, Uncertainty, Optimization, Learning, Neural Nets, Language

whatYouLearned: How Machine Learning and Deep Learning relate to AI
The difference between Narrow AI and General AI
Real examples of AI you encounter every day
The 7 core AI topics in this course
How AI systems collect data, train models, and make predictions
